This component displays a confirmation bar at the bottom of the screen when a bash command is pending approval. It shows the command description and code, along with buttons to approve or decline the execution.
This function retrieves the chat history array from local storage using a specific key. It parses the JSON string into an array, returning an empty array if the data is missing or invalid.
This component renders a fixed-position chat popover that displays a list of messages with auto-scrolling to the bottom. It also handles closing the popover when the user clicks outside of it.
This function formats a timestamp into a human-readable string, returning "Today" or "Yesterday" if applicable. Otherwise, it returns the full date formatted with weekday, month, and day.
class::frontend/src/components/molecules: This interface defines the props for the ChatTopBar component. It specifies a single property named visibleDate of type number.
function::frontend/src/components/molecu: This component renders a sticky top bar containing a conversation title, a formatted date, and a new conversation button. It uses the formatDate function to display the date in a user-friendly format.
class::frontend/src/components/molecules: This interface defines the props for the ConversationItem component. It includes properties for title, date, folder, onClick handler, and onDelete handler.
function::frontend/src/components/molecu: This component renders a clickable conversation list item displaying the title, formatted date, and optional folder tag. It includes a delete button that triggers the onDelete callback while preventing event propagation.
This component displays the current conversation title or the first message snippet if no title exists. It allows the user to edit the title inline and saves the changes via an API call.
class::frontend/src/components/molecules: This interface defines the TypeScript props required for the CreateKeyModal component. It specifies types for the open state, an onClose callback, and an onCreated callback.
This interface defines the props required for the ImageThumbnail component, including source, filename, size, and click handler. It ensures type safety for the image display data.
function::frontend/src/components/molecu: This function renders a clickable image thumbnail with hover effects and styling. It also displays the filename and file size below the image.
class::frontend/src/components/molecules: This interface defines the props for the JournalLineRow component, including line data, index, accounts, and handlers. It specifies the structure for editing a single journal entry row.
function::frontend/src/components/molecu: This function renders a table row for editing a journal line with account selection and amount inputs. It handles debit/credit logic and provides a remove button.
This function updates the debit value for the current line index. It also clears the credit value if the debit is greater than zero.
This component renders a modal for creating an API key, handling the POST request to the server. It displays the generated key with a copy-to-clipboard feature and resets state on close.

This component renders a button that toggles a dropdown menu containing a grid of emojis. It handles click-outside events to close the menu and calls the onEmojiSelect callback when an emoji is chosen.
class::frontend/src/components/molecules: This interface defines the TypeScript type for the GroupCard component's props. It specifies fields for name, tooltip, tools list, active status, and a toggle function.
function::frontend/src/components/molecu: This component renders a card displaying a group name, tooltip, and tool count. It allows toggling the active state of the group and expanding a section to list the contained tools.
This function updates the credit value for a specific journal line index. It also sets the debit value to zero if the new credit value is greater than zero.
class::frontend/src/components/molecules: This interface defines the type for the component's props. It requires a single property named message of type Message.
function::frontend/src/components/molecu: This function renders an inline gallery component with a specific payload and column layout. It includes a button in the left rail that allows the user to open the gallery in the workspace.
This function extracts gallery data from a tool call pair result. It returns null if the data is missing or invalid, otherwise it returns the items and caption.
This function renders a text bubble with markdown content and role-specific styling. It includes an optional expand button to toggle the maximum height of the text container.

This component renders a message bubble by interleaving text content with tool call results and gallery payloads based on their content offset. It also handles displaying a file preview modal when a user clicks on a file path within a tool result.
This component renders a dropdown menu for selecting an AI model from a list of available options. It displays loading states and prevents interaction while a model swap is in progress.
class::frontend/src/components/molecules: This interface defines the props required for the ParamTable component, including parameters, interactivity flag, values, and an onChange handler. It ensures type safety for the table's input data and event handling.
function::frontend/src/components/molecu: This component renders a table displaying tool parameter details such as name, type, and requirement status. It provides interactive input fields for editing values if the interactive prop is true, otherwise it shows descriptions.
class::frontend/src/components/molecules: This interface defines the props for the SaveConversationModal component. It includes state for open/close, and callbacks for save and discard actions.
function::frontend/src/components/molecu: This component renders a modal for saving a conversation with a title input. It handles saving the conversation or discarding changes via button clicks.
class::frontend/src/components/molecules: This interface defines the props for the ThinkingIndicator component. It includes a label, elapsed time, and preview text.
function::frontend/src/components/molecu: This component renders a thinking indicator with animated dots. It displays the label, elapsed time, and optional preview text.

This component renders a badge displaying the count of selected tools and manages a hover-activated popup. The popup lists the selected tool names and provides a close button to remove each individual tool.
class::frontend/src/components/molecules: This interface defines the props for the TowerAd component. It specifies a single property named side which can be either left or right.
function::frontend/src/components/molecu: This component renders a vertical advertisement sidebar with a fixed width and height. It displays a promotional message encouraging users to upgrade to Pro and includes a link to billing settings.
class::frontend/src/components/molecules: This interface defines the structure for usage status data. It contains a single numeric property named percent.
function::frontend/src/components/molecu: This function determines the highest threshold value from a predefined list that is less than or equal to the input percentage. It returns null if the input percentage is below all defined thresholds.
class::frontend/src/components/molecules: This interface defines the props for the ToolCallBlock component, including a tool call pair, a file click handler, and a bubble height value. It ensures type safety for the data passed into the component.
function::frontend/src/components/molecu: This function renders an SVG component representing a right circle with a rectangular cutout. It calculates the path data for the circle and the cutout based on predefined dimensions.

This component renders a visual block representing a tool call, including a left icon, a status bar, and a right circle. It handles expansion logic to display a hierarchy of nodes if the user clicks the block and nodes are available.
class::frontend/src/components/molecules: This interface defines the props for the ToolChip component, including a list of selected items and a remove handler. It specifies the types for the selected array and the function to remove an item by name.
function::frontend/src/components/molecu: This function generates a unique storage key string based on the current date. It ensures that dismissed usage states are isolated per day.
This function retrieves the set of dismissed thresholds from local storage using the generated key. It returns an empty set if the data is missing or invalid.
This function saves the provided set of dismissed thresholds to local storage. It serializes the set into a JSON string before storing it.
class::frontend/src/components/molecules: This interface defines the expected properties for the UsageBar component. It specifies that the component requires a usage object of type UsageStatus.
function::frontend/src/components/molecu: This component renders a visual bar indicating the current daily usage percentage against a limit. It handles dismissing the notification and persists that state to local storage for the current day.
This function adds the current threshold to a set of dismissed items. It then saves this updated set and updates the local state.
This component renders a user avatar button that toggles a dropdown menu. The menu contains navigation links to the dashboard and a logout option.
function::frontend/src/components/organi: This component fetches account data and displays it in a table with loading and error states. It renders account details such as name, type, normal balance, and number.
class::frontend/src/components/organisms: This interface defines the structure for a trial balance line item. It includes properties for account name, type, debit, and credit values.
function::frontend/src/components/organi: This component fetches and displays a trial balance table for a specific date. It includes a date picker, refresh button, and totals for debits and credits.

This component renders the main accounting workspace interface with a header, navigation tabs, and content area. It manages the active tab state to display either the journal entry form, accounts panel, or trial balance panel.
class::frontend/src/components/organisms: This interface defines the structure for the gallery payload, containing an array of items and an optional caption. It serves as a type definition for the data passed to the gallery component.

This interface defines the properties required by the ApGallery component, including payload, column count, and optional callbacks. It also specifies optional initial selection state and a left rail component.
function::frontend/src/components/organi: This component renders a selectable gallery of items with options to select all, clear, or submit the selection. It constructs a message with the selected items' details and sends it via the chat interface.
class::frontend/src/components/organisms: This code defines a TypeScript interface named ApiKey. It specifies the structure for API key data including id, prefix, label, and timestamps.
function::frontend/src/components/organi: This React component renders a panel to manage API keys, including a list view and a create button. It handles fetching keys from the API, displaying them in a table, and triggering a modal for creation.

This component renders a panel for MCP servers with filtering capabilities for tier and category. It handles loading states, errors, and displays a list of server rows or a message if no servers match.
This code maps over the servers array to render a ServerRow component for each server. It uses the server ID as the unique key for the list item.
This component displays a searchable and paginated list of conversations. It allows users to delete conversations and navigate to specific conversation details.
class::frontend/src/components/organisms: This interface defines the props for the DashboardNav component. It specifies an active section string and a callback function for selection events.
function::frontend/src/components/organi: This function renders a navigation sidebar with a back link and a list of menu items. It highlights the currently active item and triggers the onSelect callback when an item is clicked.
class::frontend/src/components/organisms: This interface defines the structure for a dropped image object. It includes fields for the file path, preview URL, and filename.
function::frontend/src/components/organi: This component renders a modal overlay to display an image and its caption. It handles closing the modal via the Escape key or clicking outside the image area.
class::frontend/src/components/organisms: This interface defines the props required for the MessageList component. It specifies a callback function for handling image click events.
function::frontend/src/components/organi: This component renders a scrollable list of chat messages with auto-scrolling and a thinking indicator. It also manages scroll position tracking and displays a button to jump to the current message.
This component displays a user profile panel with options to update username and email. It also includes a section to change the password if the user is authenticated locally.
This function removes a line from the state array at the specified index. It updates the UI by filtering out the item at that position.
This function adds a new empty line to the existing lines array. It creates a copy of the previous state and appends a new empty line object.

This function handles the form submission by preventing default behavior and checking if submission is allowed. It sends the journal entry data to the server and updates the UI with success or error messages.
This function resets the form state to its initial empty values. It clears the memo, error, and success messages while resetting the lines to two empty entries.
class::frontend/src/components/organisms: This interface defines the required properties for the Lightbox component. It specifies types for the image source, caption text, and a close callback function.
function::frontend/src/components/organi: This function uploads a file to the server via a POST request and returns the image path and preview URL. It handles errors by throwing an exception with the HTTP status or error message if the request fails.

This component renders a chat input bar that supports image drag-and-drop uploads and integrates with chat context controls. It manages drag state, triggers image uploads, and passes the resulting image data to the ChatInput component.
class::frontend/src/components/organisms: This interface defines the props for the JournalEntryForm component. It includes an optional onSuccess callback that receives the result of the journal entry operation.
function::frontend/src/components/organi: This component renders a form for creating double-entry journal entries with account selection and debit/credit inputs. It handles form submission, validates balance, and manages loading or error states for ledger creation.
This function updates a specific field in a journal line at the given index. It also clears any existing error or success messages when the line changes.
class::frontend/src/components/organisms: This interface defines the shape for the GateBlock component's props. It specifies a gate type and an onAccepted callback function.
function::frontend/src/components/organi: This component renders a security research tools waiver form with a checkbox and submit button. It handles user acceptance and calls the onAccepted callback upon successful submission.
This component renders an age verification form requiring users to confirm they are 18 or older. It handles user acceptance and calls the onAccepted callback upon successful submission.
This component acts as a router that renders either the WaiverGate or AgeGate component. It determines which gate to display based on the provided gate prop value.
class::frontend/src/components/organisms: This interface defines the props required for the TaskList component, including sidebar expansion state and CSS styles. It serves as a type definition for the component's input parameters.
function::frontend/src/components/organi: This component manages a list of tasks within a conversation, supporting creation, editing, deletion, and status toggling. It also handles drag-and-drop interactions, draft inputs, and displays a visual progress indicator.
This component provides a searchable list of tool groups from the workspace. It filters groups based on user input and renders them as cards.
This component renders the application header with the logo, model selector, theme switcher, and user menu. It provides global navigation and settings controls.
function::frontend/src/hooks/useAuth.ts:: This function retrieves the authentication context from the React context provider. It throws an error if the context is not available, ensuring it is used within the correct provider.
function::frontend/src/hooks/useChat.ts:: This function retrieves the chat context from the React context provider. It throws an error if the context is not available, ensuring it is used within the correct provider.
This interface defines the structure for the MCP servers state, including servers, categories, and loading status. It also includes fields for the timestamp and any potential error messages.
function::frontend/src/hooks/useMcpServe: This hook manages the state and loading logic for fetching MCP servers based on provided filters. It returns the current state along with a reload function to refresh the data.
This function updates the component state by setting the loading flag to true and clearing any existing error. It preserves the previous state values while applying these specific changes.
function::frontend/src/components/organi: This component fetches and displays restricted tool groups, handling gate status checks for waiver and age restrictions. It allows users to toggle group activation and refreshes the workspace state upon accepting gates.
class::frontend/src/components/organisms: This interface defines the props required for the Sidebar component, including an expanded boolean state and a toggle function. It ensures type safety for the sidebar's interactive properties.
function::frontend/src/components/organi: This component renders a collapsible sidebar containing a tool explorer and a toggle chevron icon. It applies conditional styling and blur effects based on the expanded state.
This function renders an SVG icon representing a checklist with three rows indicating checked, in-progress, and pending statuses. It uses specific colors for each status to visually distinguish them.
This function builds a dependency map from a list of tasks, mapping each task ID to its dependent tasks. It returns a Map where keys are parent task IDs and values are arrays of child task IDs.
function::frontend/src/hooks/useModels.t: This function retrieves the current model context from the React context provider. It throws an error if the hook is used outside of a valid ModelProvider component.
function::frontend/src/hooks/useStream.t: This function splits the input text into lines and parses each line as a JSON stream event. It returns an array of parsed events and any remaining unparsed text.
class::frontend/src/hooks/useStream.ts::: This interface defines the callback structure for handling stream events, completion, and errors. It specifies the function signatures for onEvent, onDone, and onError handlers.
function::frontend/src/hooks/useStream.t: This hook manages an asynchronous chat stream by reading data, parsing NDJSON events, and invoking callbacks. It handles connection errors, aborts existing streams, and provides start and stop functions.
This function checks if the input is a non-array object and returns it cast as a record. It returns an empty object if the input is invalid or not an object.
This function checks if the input is an array and returns it if true. It returns an empty array if the input is not an array.
This function checks if the input is a string and returns it if true. It returns the provided fallback value if the input is not a string.
This function checks if the input is a number and returns it if true. It returns the provided fallback value if the input is not a number.
This function attempts to parse the input string as a URL and extract the hostname without the www prefix. It returns the original input string if parsing fails.
function::frontend/src/hooks/useTheme.ts: This function retrieves the current theme context from the React context provider. It throws an error if the context is not available, ensuring it is used within a ThemeProvider.
function::frontend/src/hooks/useTools.ts: This function retrieves the current tool context from the React context provider. It throws an error if the context is not available, ensuring it is used within a ToolProvider.
function::frontend/src/hooks/useWebSocke: This function retrieves the current WebSocket context from the React context provider. It throws an error if the context is not available, ensuring it is used within a WebSocketProvider.
function::frontend/src/hooks/useWorkspac: This function retrieves the current workspace context from the React context provider. It throws an error if the context is not available, ensuring it is used within a WorkspaceProvider.
class::frontend/src/lib/toolAdapters.ts:: This interface defines a contract for tool adapters with methods for summarization and hierarchy conversion. It specifies the expected input parameters and return types for these operations.
This function extracts the text content following a specific pattern of a greater-than sign, digits, and whitespace from a snippet string. It returns the trimmed extracted text if the pattern matches, otherwise it returns the original trimmed snippet.

This function parses a string representation of a directory tree into an array of hierarchy nodes by calculating depth based on indentation. It constructs full paths for each node and determines whether it is a file or directory based on the trailing slash.
This function generates a summary string for a journal entry using its ID and memo. It formats the output to display the entry number and the memo text.
This function converts the input data into a hierarchy of journal stanza nodes. It delegates the processing to the journalStanzaNodes helper function.

This function converts an input value to a number and formats it as a US locale string with exactly two decimal places. It handles both string and numeric inputs by parsing them appropriately before formatting.

This function generates a hierarchical list of nodes representing a journal entry, including its ID, date, memo, and formatted line items. It calculates debits and credits, pads account names for alignment, and appends a separator line and total sum at the end.
This function retrieves a tool adapter from the ADAPTERS map using the provided tool name. It returns null if no adapter is found for the given name.
function::frontend/src/pages/ChatPage.ts: This component renders the main chat interface with a sidebar, message list, and input bar, supporting different layout modes. It handles conversation loading, image lightbox display, and workspace gallery views.
function::frontend/src/pages/CliAuthPage: This component displays a CLI authentication page that allows users to approve or deny access requests. It handles the authentication flow, shows status messages, and redirects to login if not authenticated.
function::frontend/src/pages/DashboardPa: This component renders the main dashboard layout with a sidebar navigation and a dynamic content area. It manages the active section state to display the corresponding panel component.
function::frontend/src/pages/LoginPage.t: This component renders a login and registration form with input fields for username, email, and password. It handles form submission, validation, and OAuth redirects for GitHub and Google.
function::frontend/src/pages/PrivacyPage: This helper component renders a styled section with a title and content. It applies specific typography and spacing classes for the privacy policy layout.
This helper component renders a string as a styled code block. It applies monospace font and background styling for file paths or technical terms.
This component renders the full privacy policy page with multiple sections detailing data usage and storage. It includes the application branding and a particle background canvas.
function::frontend/src/pages/SupportPage: This component renders a styled section with a title and children content. It applies specific CSS classes for typography and spacing.
This component renders the main support page layout with a glassmorphism card. It displays contact information, troubleshooting steps, and uninstall instructions.
class::frontend/src/providers/AuthProvid: This interface defines the shape of the authentication context value. It includes user state, loading status, error message, and auth functions.
function::frontend/src/providers/AuthPro: This component manages authentication state and provides it via context. It handles login, registration, logout, and initial user fetching.
class::frontend/src/providers/ChatProvid: This interface defines the structure for a bash command confirmation prompt. It contains the command string and a description string.
class::frontend/src/providers/ModelProvi: This interface defines the shape of the model context value, including lists of models, current selection, and state flags. It also declares the function signature for selecting a model.
function::frontend/src/providers/ModelPr: This component manages the state for available models and the currently selected model by fetching data from the API. It provides this state and a selection function to its children via the ModelContext provider.
class::frontend/src/providers/Preference: This interface defines the structure for the preferences context, including the preferences object and an update function. It ensures type safety for components consuming this context.
function::frontend/src/providers/Prefere: This component manages user preferences state and syncs updates to the backend with a debounced flush mechanism. It provides the current preferences and an update function to its children via the PreferencesContext provider.
This custom hook retrieves the current preferences context value. It throws an error if used outside of a PreferencesProvider component.
class::frontend/src/providers/ChatProvid: This interface defines the shape of the chat context value, including messages, streaming state, and conversation ID. It also declares function signatures for sending messages, canceling streams, clearing history, and handling bash command confirmations.
function::frontend/src/providers/ChatPro: This component manages the chat state, including messages, streaming status, and conversation ID, while handling model changes and conversation loading. It implements the sendMessage function to handle user input, stream tokens, tool calls, and auto-summarization when context limits are approached.

This function updates the message list to include a new tool execution pair with a streaming status. It either appends the pair to the last assistant message or creates a new assistant message if one does not exist.
class::frontend/src/providers/ThemeProvi: This interface defines the structure for the theme context value, including the current theme, a setter function, and a list of available themes. It ensures type safety for the theme-related data passed through the React context.
function::frontend/src/providers/ThemePr: This component manages the application's theme state by initializing it from local storage or a default value. It provides a context to children that allows them to access the current theme and change it while persisting the selection.
class::frontend/src/providers/ToolProvid: This interface defines the shape of the tool context value, including categories, selected tools, and functions to toggle or refresh tools. It serves as a type definition for the data and actions available in the tool context.
function::frontend/src/providers/ToolPro: This helper function extracts the names of all selected tools from the provided categories. It filters tools by their selected status and maps them to their names.

This component fetches tool categories from the API on mount and manages their state. It provides a context to children with the current tools, selected tools, and functions to toggle or refresh the tool list.
class::frontend/src/providers/WebSocketP: This interface defines the shape of the WebSocket context value, including connection status and a method to send tool results. It specifies the types for the `connected` boolean and the `sendToolResult` function signature.
This interface defines the props required by the WebSocketProvider component, including children, an enabled flag, and an optional URL. It ensures type safety for the component's input parameters.
function::frontend/src/providers/WebSock: This component manages a WebSocket connection to the server, updating the connection state based on open, close, and error events. It provides a context value with the connection status and a function to send tool results to the backend.
class::frontend/src/providers/WorkspaceP: This interface defines the structure of the workspace context, including layout state, groups, active groups, and various UI toggle functions. It serves as a type definition for the shared state and actions within the workspace.
function::frontend/src/providers/Workspa: This component manages the workspace state, including loading workflow groups, toggling group activity, and handling UI layout changes. It provides a context with all necessary state variables and functions to the rest of the application.
function::installer/atomic_chat_installe: This function enables ANSI color support on Windows systems by calling the Windows API. It silently ignores any errors that occur during the process.
This function prints a message to the console with a green prefix and a plus sign. It is used for displaying informational output.
This function prints a message to the console with a yellow prefix and an exclamation mark. It is used for displaying warning messages.
This function prints a message to the console with a cyan bold arrow prefix. It is used for displaying step indicators.
This function prints an error message to the console with a red prefix and an x mark. It then exits the program with a status code of 1.
This function sends an HTTP GET request to the specified URL with a custom User-Agent header. It parses and returns the response body as a JSON object.
This function downloads a file from the given URL to the specified destination path. It displays a real-time progress bar indicating the percentage of data downloaded.
This function iterates through a range of port numbers to find one that is available for binding. It returns the first free port found or fails if no ports are available in the range.
This function executes a system command with the provided arguments and captures its output. It raises an error if the command fails and the check flag is enabled.
This function searches for a valid Python executable among common commands like python, python3, and py. It verifies that the found executable supports Python version 3.12 or higher.
This function checks if the Node.js runtime is installed and accessible on the system. It returns True if the version command executes successfully, otherwise it returns False.

This function attempts to install the uv package manager if it is not already present on the system. It handles installation commands for both Windows and Unix-like environments and returns the path to the executable.
This function retrieves the total physical RAM installed on the system in gigabytes. It uses Windows-specific ctypes calls to query memory status and returns 0 if an error occurs.
class::installer/atomic_chat_installer.p: This class defines a ctypes structure to hold memory status information from the Windows API. It maps specific fields for total physical memory and other memory metrics.
function::installer/atomic_chat_installe: This function detects the available GPU acceleration backend on a Windows system. It checks for NVIDIA, AMD, or Intel GPUs and returns the appropriate backend string like cuda, vulkan, or cpu.
This function scans a predefined list of directories for existing GGUF model files. It returns a sorted list of all found model file paths.

This function fetches the latest llama.cpp release from GitHub and selects the appropriate asset based on the specified GPU type. It downloads, extracts, and returns the path to the llama-server.exe executable.

This function retrieves a list of GGUF files from a Hugging Face repository and allows the user to select one if multiple are present. It downloads the selected file to the specified output directory with a progress bar.

This function checks if the application source code already exists in the target directory. If not, it downloads the latest release zip from GitHub, extracts it, and renames the folder to the target directory.
This function initializes the database by running a Python command within the application directory. It sets the DATABASE_URL environment variable and reports success or failure.

This function orchestrates the local installation by checking for and installing Python, Node.js, and llama-server dependencies. It then configures the model, GPU settings, conversation storage, and environment variables for the full local stack.

This function sets up a cloud client installation by ensuring Python is present and copying the necessary agent source code. It installs required dependencies via uv or pip and prompts the user for an API key to write to the client environment file.

This function creates a desktop shortcut on Windows using PowerShell and the WScript.Shell COM object. It handles the target path, description, and optional icon location while catching any errors during creation.

This function displays the setup wizard banner and prompts the user to choose between a full local stack or a cloud client only installation. It then calls the corresponding installation function based on the user's input.
function::installer/build_msix.py::_info: This function prints a formatted informational message to the console with a specific prefix. It ensures the output is flushed immediately to prevent buffering issues.
function::installer/build_msix.py::_fail: This function prints an error message to stderr and exits the program with a status code of 1. It is used to terminate the build process when a critical error occurs.
function::installer/build_msix.py::_reso: This function attempts to retrieve the version string from a CLI argument or the pyproject.toml file. It returns a default version of 0.0.0 if neither source provides a valid value.
function::installer/build_msix.py::_stam: This function updates the Identity Version attribute in an MSIX manifest file to a four-part version string. It uses regex to preserve original namespace prefixes and fails if the attribute is missing.
function::installer/build_msix.py::_resi: This function resizes and crops a source PNG image to fit exact target dimensions using the Pillow library. It ensures the output image is centered and saved in PNG format.
function::installer/build_msix.py::_stag: This function creates a staging directory and populates it with resized PNG assets based on a predefined list. It verifies that all source assets exist before processing them.
function::main.py::_tool_meta: This method extracts the name, description, and parameter schema from a qwen-agent tool class. It formats the parameter information into a dictionary containing type, description, required status, and default values.
function::main.py::register_auth_bps: This function initializes the login manager and OAuth components for the application. It then registers multiple blueprints for various routes and sets up a before-request authentication guard.
function::main.py::list_workflows: This function defines a GET route for the /api/workflows endpoint. It is intended to handle requests for listing available workflows.
function::installer/build_msix.py::_buil: This function attempts to build the agent executable using PyInstaller if the current host is Windows. It returns the expected path to the executable and skips the build process on non-Windows systems.
function::installer/build_msix.py::_stag: This function prepares the staging directory by copying the manifest and assets, then stamps the version number. It also copies the built agent executable into the staging directory if it exists.
function::installer/build_msix.py::_find: This function searches for the makeappx executable in the system PATH and common Windows SDK directories. It returns the full path to the executable if found, or None if it cannot be located.
function::installer/build_msix.py::main: This function orchestrates the build process by staging the package and attempting to pack it into an MSIX file. It writes a summary JSON file with the results and next steps to the distribution directory.
function::main.py::serve_frontend: This function defines a route for the root URL with a default empty path. It serves as the entry point for the frontend application.
class::pipeline/workflow_groups.py::Work: This class represents a workflow group with support for lazy tool discovery based on a name prefix. It merges explicitly provided tools with any discovered tools from the registry that match the prefix.
function::pipeline/workflow_groups.py::_: This method initializes the WorkflowGroup instance with a tooltip, optional prefix, tools list, and gate. It stores these values as instance attributes, defaulting the tools list to an empty list if not provided.
function::pipeline/workflow_groups.py::t: This property returns a list of tool names associated with the workflow group. If a prefix is set, it dynamically discovers additional tools from the registry that start with that prefix and merges them with the explicit list.
class::pipeline/workflow_groups.py::_Laz: This class provides a lazy-loading, dict-like interface to access tool descriptions from a global registry. It caches the first line of each tool's description to ensure a single source of truth.
function::pipeline/workflow_groups.py::_: This method initializes the cache by extracting the first line of the description for every tool in the registry. It returns the cached dictionary of tool names to descriptions.
function::pipeline/workflow_groups.py::i: This method returns the items of the built tool description dictionary. It allows iteration over key-value pairs of tool names and descriptions.
function::pipeline/workflow_groups.py::k: This method returns the keys of the built tool description dictionary. It provides an iterable view of all available tool names.
function::pipeline/workflow_groups.py::v: This method returns the values from the built object. It delegates the operation to the internal _build method.
function::pipeline/workflow_groups.py::_: This method retrieves an item by key from the built object. It delegates the lookup to the internal _build method.
This method checks if a key exists in the built object. It delegates the containment check to the internal _build method.
This method returns an iterator for the built object. It delegates the iteration to the internal _build method.
This method returns the length of the built object. It delegates the length calculation to the internal _build method.
function::pipeline/workflow_groups.py::t: This function retrieves a compact reference string for the tools within a specified group. It returns an empty string if the group does not exist.
function::pipeline/workflow_groups.py::b: This function builds a formatted tool reference string with parameters and descriptions, grouped by category. It retrieves tool metadata from the registry to include required and optional parameters.
function::pipeline/workflow_groups.py::t: This function returns a flat list of tool names corresponding to the provided group names. It iterates through the groups and extends the result list with their tools.
function::routes/accounting.py::_db: This function returns a new database session instance. It serves as a factory for creating local sessions.
function::routes/accounting.py::accounts: This function retrieves the list of accounts for the current user by calling the implementation function. It returns the result as a JSON response and ensures the database connection is closed.

function::routes/accounting.py::trial_ba: This function retrieves the trial balance up to a specified date for the current user. It returns the result as a JSON response and closes the database connection.
function::routes/bridge.py::get_pubkey: This function serves the server's RSA public key for client challenge-response authentication. It returns the key as plain text with a 200 status code.
function::routes/bridge.py::bridge_conne: This function establishes a persistent WebSocket connection and performs mutual authentication using a challenge-response protocol with RSA-OAEP encryption. It then enters a loop to handle incoming tool results and ping messages while managing the connection registration.
class::routes/chat.py::DynamicAssistant: This class extends FnCallAgent to support dynamic injection of tools into the function map based on the need_tool response. It also initializes a custom Bash tool that is wired to a web UI confirmation queue for user approval of commands.
function::routes/chat.py::__init__: This method initializes the class by storing the conversation ID and bash interrupt queue. It then calls the parent class constructor with the remaining arguments.
function::routes/chat.py::_make_bash_too: This method creates and returns a custom BashTool subclass that is wired to the web UI confirmation queue. It overrides the confirm method to wait for user approval via the queue or fall back to standard input.
class::routes/chat.py::_BashWithCtx: This inner class extends BashTool to override the confirmation logic for executing commands. It attempts to get approval from the web UI queue and falls back to a command-line prompt if the queue is not available.
function::routes/chat.py::_confirm: This function handles command confirmation by either waiting for an asynchronous approval event or prompting the user for input synchronously. It returns a boolean indicating whether the command was approved to run.
function::routes/chat.py::_call_tool: This method executes a tool call and checks if the result indicates a need for a new tool. If a new tool is required, it dynamically registers it into the function map and sets its time-to-live.
function::routes/chat.py::_llama_error_m: This function traverses an exception chain to extract a human-readable error message related to the llama server. It provides specific feedback for common issues like connection errors, timeouts, or context overflow.
function::routes/chat.py::_str: This helper function converts an exception object to a lowercase string. It is used internally to normalize error messages for pattern matching.
class::routes/chat.py::_BashConfirm: This class acts as a sentinel object to signal that the bash tool requires confirmation from the web UI. It stores the associated data dictionary for the confirmation request.
function::routes/chat.py::_pump: This function defines a background thread that processes an iterable and puts items into a queue. It handles errors and signals completion by putting specific markers into the queue.
function::routes/chat.py::chat_stream: This function defines a POST route for the /stream endpoint. It is part of the chat blueprint.
function::routes/chat.py::generate: This function commits the database transaction. It ensures data changes are saved.
function::routes/chat.py::_log: This function yields a JSON string containing the context percentage. It appends a newline character to the output.
function::routes/chat.py::bash_confirm: This function defines a POST route for the /bash_confirm endpoint. It is part of the chat blueprint.
function::routes/chat.py::chat_cancel: This function defines a POST route for the /cancel endpoint. It is part of the chat blueprint.
function::routes/chat.py::summarize_cont: This function defines a POST route for summarizing context. It is decorated with the chat blueprint route.
function::routes/conversations.py::creat: This function creates a new conversation entry in the store using data from the request JSON. It returns a JSON response with the created conversation or an error if storage is disabled.
function::routes/conversations.py::list_: This function retrieves a list of conversations for the current user with optional filtering and pagination. It returns the resulting list as a JSON response.
function::routes/files.py::_safe_path: This function validates a file path by checking for empty strings, directory traversal attempts, and ensuring it resides within allowed root directories. It returns the resolved absolute path if valid, or None if the path is unsafe or invalid.
function::routes/files.py::read_file: This function reads the content of a specified file, ensuring the path is safe and the file is not an image. function::routes/files.py::upload_file: This function handles file uploads by validating the presence of the file and checking its extension against an allowed list. It saves the file with a unique UUID-based name to the upload directory and returns the saved path.
function::routes/health.py::_check_db: This function checks database connectivity by executing a simple SELECT 1 query and measuring the execution latency. It returns a tuple indicating success, the latency in milliseconds, and any error message if the query fails.
function::routes/health.py::_check_llama: This function probes the llama-server health endpoint to check if the service is alive and measure the response latency. It returns a tuple with the health status, latency, and the loaded model ID if the server is healthy.

function::routes/health.py::ready: This function checks the health status of the database and llama server dependencies. It returns a JSON payload with the status and latency details, along with an appropriate HTTP status code.

function::routes/preferences.py::change_: This function validates the current password and ensures the new password meets length requirements. It then hashes the new password and saves it to the database for local accounts.

class::tools/khan.py::KhanListCourses: This class defines a tool that lists the courses on the user's Khan Academy dashboard. It extracts course names, URLs, and topics from the dashboard page.
class::tools/khan.py::KhanUnitFetch: This class defines a tool that fetches a Khan Academy unit page and returns the unit description and list of lessons. It extracts lesson names and URLs from the page.
function::tools/khan.py::_detect_page_ty: This function detects the type of page (video, article, or unknown) based on the presence of specific elements. It checks for a video play button or content library title.
function::tools/khan.py::_extract_video_: This function extracts the transcript from a video page by parsing the HTML. It skips timestamps and returns the text segments joined by spaces.
function::tools/khan.py::_extract_articl: This function extracts the title and all paragraph text from a Khan Academy article page using BeautifulSoup. It handles sub-headings and loose text nodes to construct a list of clean text segments.
function::tools/khan.py::_find_next_less: This function attempts to find the URL of the next lesson by parsing the specific footer structure of the page. It falls back to searching for any link containing "next" or "continue" if the specific structure is not found.
class::tools/khan.py::KhanLessonScrape: This class defines a tool that scrapes a Khan Academy lesson page to extract either a video transcript or article content. It also attempts to find the URL of the next lesson in the sequence.
function::tools/accounting.py::_search_j: This function searches for journal entries in the database based on date range, memo text, and other filters. It returns a list of matching entries with their associated line items and account details.
function::tools/accounting.py::_void_tra: This function voids a specific journal entry by creating a reversing entry with swapped debit and credit amounts. It also handles inventory adjustments if the original entry was related to inventory sales or receipts.
function::tools/accounting.py::_account_: This function retrieves all non-voided journal lines for a specific account within an optional date range. It calculates and returns the running balance for each entry along with the transaction details.
function::tools/ecommerce.py::_parse_shi: This function parses a string representing shipping cost and returns it as a float. It handles cases where the shipping is free or contains currency symbols and commas.
function::tools/ecommerce.py::_validate_: This function validates that the provided query string is not empty or just whitespace. It returns an error message if the query is invalid, otherwise it returns None.
function::installer/build_msix.py::_pack: This function uses the makeappx tool to package a staged directory into an MSIX file. It checks for the tool's availability and handles errors if the packaging process fails.
function::services/llama.py::_read_pid: This function reads a process ID from a file and checks if the process is still running. It returns the PID if the process exists, or None if the file is missing or the process is not found.
function::services/logging_setup.py::set: This function sets a correlation ID for the current async or thread context. It uses a default value of "-" if the provided value is empty or null.
function::tools/khan_cookie_ext/backgrou: This function retrieves all cookies for a specified domain and maps them to a simplified object format. It sends the resulting list and count back as a response or handles any errors that occur.
This code snippet catches any errors thrown in the preceding promise chain. It sends the error message string back as a response to the caller.
This function creates a script element to inject cookie data into the window object. It also dispatches a custom event to notify the page of the update.
This function sends a message to the background script to request cookies for a specific domain. It calls injectCookies with the received data if available.
This function requests all domains from the background script. It iterates through the domains and fetches cookies for each one.
function::frontend/src/App.tsx::AuthGate: This component handles authentication checks and redirects to specific pages like login or CLI auth. It renders the main application layout with providers if the user is authenticated.
function::frontend/src/App.tsx::App: This component serves as the root of the application. It wraps the AuthGate component with error boundary, router, auth, and theme providers.
function::frontend/src/providers/ToolPro: This function toggles a specific category by calling the API. It updates the local categories state with the response data.
This function fetches the latest tools from the API. It updates the local categories state with the fetched data.
class::frontend/src/providers/CookieProv: This interface defines the shape of the cookie context value, including store, status, and actions. It specifies types for cookie data and functions to sync or refresh cookies.
This interface extends the global Window object. It adds an optional property for the atomic chat cookie store.
function::frontend/src/providers/CookieP: This function flattens a nested cookie store object into a single array of cookie entries. It iterates through domains and filters out invalid entries.
This function reads the cookie store from the global window object. It returns null if the store is missing or not ready, otherwise it returns a copy.
This component manages the cookie state and provides it via context. It handles initialization, polling, event listening, syncing, and refreshing of cookies.
This code snippet updates the cookie store state with new cookies for a specific domain. It merges the new data into the previous state.
This function syncs the current cookie store to the backend. It updates the last synced count and handles errors silently.
This function dispatches a refresh event for each domain in the cookie store. It triggers the extension to update the cookies for those domains.
This function retrieves the current cookie context from the React context provider. It throws an error if the context is not available, ensuring the hook is used within a valid provider.
function::frontend/src/hooks/useStream.t: This function initiates an asynchronous chat stream by aborting any existing stream and reading data chunks. It parses NDJSON events, handles errors with specific messages, and invokes callbacks for events, completion, or errors.
This function aborts the currently active stream if one exists. It uses a callback wrapper to ensure the abort function is called safely.
function::frontend/src/hooks/useMcpServe: This function fetches MCP server data asynchronously and updates the state with the results. It handles loading states and catches errors, storing the error message in the state if the request fails.
function::frontend/src/api/cookies.ts::s: This function synchronizes the current cookie store with the backend by sending a POST request. It filters out invalid entries and returns the result or throws an error if the request fails.
function::frontend/src/providers/Prefere: This function sends the pending preference patch to the API to persist changes. It clears the pending patch on success or re-queues it on failure, handling errors gracefully.
This function updates the local preferences state and queues a patch for persistence. It debounces the flush operation to avoid excessive API calls.
function::frontend/src/providers/ToolPro: This function toggles the state of a specific tool by calling the API. It updates the local categories state with the response data if available.
function::frontend/src/providers/AuthPro: This function attempts to log in a user by calling the apiLogin function with the provided username and password. It updates the local user state if successful or sets an error message if the login fails.

This function attempts to register a new user by calling the apiRegister function with the provided credentials. It updates the local user state if successful or sets an error message if the registration fails.
This function calls the apiLogout function to perform server-side logout actions. It then clears the local user state by setting the user to null.
function::frontend/src/providers/ModelPr: This function sets a swapping flag to true and calls the apiSelectModel function with the provided model. It updates the current model state upon success and ensures the swapping flag is reset in the finally block.
function::frontend/src/providers/Workspa: This function fetches workflow groups from the API and updates the local groups state with the result. It catches and logs any errors that occur during the fetch operation.

This function toggles the active status of a specified group in the local state and adjusts the layout accordingly. It then attempts to sync this change with the backend and refresh tools, logging any errors while preserving UI state.
This function updates the selected tool state with the provided name. It serves as a callback to handle tool selection events.
This function sets the gallery payload with the provided data. It also updates the layout to workspace-chat if it was previously set to default.
This function clears the gallery payload by setting it to null. It resets the layout to default if no active groups are present, otherwise it preserves the current layout.
function::frontend/src/providers/WebSock: This function sends a JSON stringified message containing a tool result to the WebSocket connection. It uses the provided id and output to construct the message payload.
function::frontend/src/providers/ChatPro: This function fetches conversation data by ID and maps the raw messages into the application's internal message format. It then updates the state with the loaded messages and sets the current conversation ID.
This function initializes a new conversation by clearing the messages array. It also resets the conversation ID to null.

This function handles sending a user message, managing streaming state, and setting up a watchdog timer for stale connections. It processes incoming stream events to update the UI with tokens, images, tool calls, and results, and triggers auto-summarization if context usage is high.
This function stops the current streaming process and cancels the active chat request. It resets the streaming state flags to false.
This function calls the API to clear the chat history on the server side. It then resets the local state, including messages, conversation ID, and context percentage.

This function triggers a manual context summarization if the conversation is active and not already summarizing. It updates the context percentage and reloads the conversation, handling errors by displaying a message to the user.
This function approves a pending bash command execution by clearing the confirmation state. It sends an approval response to the server for the current conversation.
This function declines a pending bash command execution by clearing the confirmation state. It sends a decline response to the server for the current conversation.
function::frontend/src/pages/ChatPage.ts: This function sets the lightbox state with the provided image source and caption. It is used to display an enlarged view of the clicked image.
function::frontend/src/components/organi: This function prevents the default browser behavior and increments a drag counter. It sets the drag over state to true if the dragged data includes files.
This function prevents the default browser behavior and decrements the drag counter. It sets the drag over state to false if the counter reaches zero.
This function prevents the default browser behavior during a drag over event. It ensures the drop target remains active without additional logic.
This function resets the drag state and validates that the dropped file is an image. It uploads the image, sets the dropped image state, and handles errors or loading states.
This function fetches API keys from the server with credentials included. It parses the JSON response and updates the keys state with the retrieved data.
This function sets a loading state and fetches account data from the server. It updates the accounts state or sets a no ledger flag based on the response status.
This function returns the full list of groups if the search query is empty. Otherwise, it filters groups and tools based on a case-insensitive match against the search query.
This function fetches a list of conversations with pagination and search parameters. It updates the conversations and total count states with the retrieved data.
This function concurrently fetches workflow groups and gate status. It updates the groups and gate status states and sets loading to false.
This function updates the gate status to true for the specified gate. It then calls the refresh groups function to update the UI.

This function fetches trial balance data for a specific date and updates the component state with rows and totals. It handles loading states and errors, ensuring the UI reflects the success or failure of the data retrieval.

This async function retrieves tasks for a given conversation ID and updates the local state with the fetched data. It defaults to the current conversation ID if none is provided and exits early if no ID is available.

This function ensures a conversation exists by returning the current ID or creating a new one if necessary. It initializes the conversation with a default title and loads its data before returning the ID.

This function submits a new task draft by creating a conversation task with the trimmed title. It removes the draft from the local state, ensures a conversation exists, and reloads the task list upon success.
This function sets the editing state with the ID and title of the specified task. It prepares the UI to allow the user to modify the task's title.
This function updates the title of an existing task using the trimmed editing value. It clears the editing state and reloads the task list to reflect the changes, handling errors if the update fails.
This function deletes a specific task from the conversation by its ID. It reloads the task list to update the UI and logs an error if the deletion fails.
This function toggles the status of a task between done and pending. It updates the task on the server, reloads the list, and handles any potential errors during the update.
class::frontend/src/components/atoms/Amo: This interface defines the props for an amount input component, including value, onChange handler, and optional styling. It also specifies optional properties for disabled state, placeholder text, and custom class names.
function::frontend/src/components/atoms/: This function renders a sticky date separator component with a formatted timestamp label. It returns null if the label is empty and applies specific styling for centering and background color.
This function renders an avatar component that displays an image if a source is provided. Otherwise, it displays a user icon inside a rounded div with specified size and styling.
function::frontend/src/components/organi: This function toggles the active state of a group by adding or removing it from the active list. It attempts to select the group via an API call and reverts the local state if the call fails.
This function detects if the user has scrolled away from the bottom of the message container. It also calculates and sets the timestamp of the message currently visible at the top of the view.
This function smoothly scrolls the message container to the bottom. It also resets the user scrolled state to false.
function::frontend/src/components/molecu: This function clears any existing timeout scheduled to close the popup. It resets the timer reference to null.
This function cancels any pending close timer and sets the popup visibility to true. It ensures the popup remains open when triggered.
This function cancels any existing close timer and schedules a new one to hide the popup after 300 milliseconds. It manages the automatic closing behavior of the component.
This function constructs a message string from text and optional image data. It updates the history, saves the draft, triggers the send action, and clears the input field.
This component renders a button to start a new conversation if there are existing messages. It returns null if the message list is empty.
This component renders a promotional banner with an upgrade link and a close button. It displays static text encouraging the user to upgrade to Pro.
class::frontend/src/api/cookies.ts::Cook: This interface defines the structure for a cookie entry with various optional properties. It includes fields for name, value, domain, and security settings.
function::routes/graph.py::neo4j_ingest: This function clears the existing Neo4j database and ensures the schema is present. It then ingests data from a graph.json file and returns a success status.
function::routes/graph.py::neo4j_status: This function checks the connection status to the Neo4j database. If connected, it returns the URI and counts of nodes and edges; otherwise, it returns an error message.
function::services/neo4j_prompt.py::buil: This function builds a system prompt by injecting relevant tool and file context retrieved from Neo4j. It combines this context with a provided base prompt and returns the final string.
This function generates a focused context snippet containing only tool information relevant to the task. It returns an empty string if no relevant tools are found.
This function creates a lightweight hint string listing the names and categories of tools relevant to the task. It returns a default message if no tools are identified.
function::services/neo4j_prompt.py::get_: This function retrieves the full context bundle for a given task, including the dynamic prompt, tool context, and hint. It also includes the raw lists of tools and related files for API consumption.
function::services/graph_query.py::_load: This function loads the graph.json file into a global cache if it is not already loaded. It also builds a tool index from the cached data and returns the cache.
function::services/graph_query.py::reloa: This function forces a reload of the graph data by clearing the global cache and tool index. It then calls the load function to repopulate the cache.
function::scripts/gexf_export.py::main: This function loads a graph from a JSON file and converts it into a GEXF XML format. It defines node attributes, iterates through nodes and edges to build the XML structure, and writes the final output to a file.
function::routes/chat.py::_build_neo4j_t: This function queries Neo4j to retrieve relevant tools and file graph context based on a task description. It formats the retrieved tools and file details, including dependencies and contents, into a structured string for the prompt.
function::routes/graph.py::graph_visuali: This endpoint returns visualization-optimized graph data by querying Neo4j with optional type and depth filters. It falls back to loading data from a local JSON file if the Neo4j query fails.
function::routes/graph.py::tool_catalog: This function returns the full tool catalog including categories and summaries as a JSON response. It retrieves the data by calling the get_tool_catalog function.
function::routes/graph.py::tool_details: This endpoint retrieves detailed information for specific tools specified by comma-separated names in the query parameters. It returns an error if no names are provided, otherwise it returns the details for the requested tools.
function::routes/graph.py::route_tools: This function scores tools against a provided task description and returns the top ranked matches. It attempts to use Neo4j for scoring but falls back to a local JSON-based scoring method if Neo4j is unavailable.
function::routes/graph.py::generate_prom: This endpoint generates a dynamic system prompt for a given task by retrieving context from Neo4j. It falls back to a legacy prompt generation method using local JSON data if the Neo4j query fails.
function::routes/graph.py::reload: This function forces a reload of the graph data from the local JSON file on disk. It returns a JSON status message confirming that the reload was successful.
function::services/neo4j_context.py::ing: This function loads a JSON graph file and ingests its nodes and edges into Neo4j in batches. It ensures the schema exists before performing the upsert operations.
function::services/neo4j_context.py::_in: This function sanitizes node data by flattening nested structures and normalizing path properties. It then executes a Cypher query to upsert these nodes into the database.
This function creates relationships between existing nodes based on the provided edge list. It uses APOC to dynamically create relationship types from the input data.
function::services/neo4j_context.py::sea: This function performs a full-text search on node properties using Neo4j's index. It returns the top matching nodes with their relevance scores, optionally filtering by node type.
function::services/neo4j_context.py::sco: This function scores tool classes against a task description using full-text search. It retrieves tool details and associated call summaries, ordered by relevance.
function::services/graph_query.py::_buil: This function extracts all tool-class nodes from the graph, filtering out TypeScript interfaces and non-class types. It constructs a list of dictionaries containing the tool's ID, name, summary, file path, and inferred category.
function::services/graph_query.py::_infe: This function infers a category string for a tool based on keywords found in its name or file path. It checks for specific domains like web, bug_bounty, exploit, or core and returns "other" if no match is found.
function::services/graph_query.py::_toke: This function extracts meaningful words from a given text string by converting it to lowercase and filtering out stop words. It returns a list of valid tokens that are at least two characters long.
function::services/graph_query.py::score: This function scores all available tools against a task description using keyword overlap, category matches, and name component matches. It returns a ranked list of the top tools based on their calculated relevance scores.
function::services/graph_query.py::get_t: This function retrieves full details for a specific list of tool names from the tool index. It returns a list of dictionaries containing the name, category, summary, call summary, and file path for each requested tool.
This function returns a dictionary mapping each tool's name to its category, summary, and call summary. It uses the existing tool index or builds it if necessary.
function::services/graph_query.py::get_v: This function returns a visualization-optimized subgraph of nodes and edges, optionally filtering by node type and depth. It strips code fields from nodes and keeps only summary and line pointer information for display purposes.
function::services/neo4j_context.py::get: This function returns a singleton Neo4j driver instance, creating it on the first call if it does not exist. It verifies connectivity and logs the connection status upon initialization.
function::services/neo4j_context.py::clo: This function closes the existing Neo4j driver connection and sets the global driver variable to None. It is intended to be called during application shutdown.
function::services/neo4j_context.py::ens: This function ensures that the required Neo4j schema constraints and full-text indexes exist by running predefined queries. It logs a confirmation message once the schema setup is complete.
function::services/neo4j_context.py::cle: This function deletes all nodes and relationships from the Neo4j database. It logs a confirmation message after the operation is complete.
function::services/graph_prompt.py::buil: This function generates a formatted string listing tools relevant to a specific task description. It groups the tools by category and includes their summaries and relevance scores.
This function constructs a complete system prompt by combining a base prompt with task-specific tool context. It includes the task description and a list of recommended tools if requested.
This function returns a dictionary containing a ranked list of tool recommendations for a given task. It includes the tool name, relevance score, and category for each recommendation.
class::frontend/src/providers/WorkspaceP: This interface defines the structure for a dependency list item with optional fields for code and file location. It allows for additional arbitrary string keys with unknown values.

This interface defines the structure for a graph visualization node including type, label, and optional file details. It includes an optional list of dependencies conforming to the DependencyListItem interface.
This interface defines the structure for graph data containing a list of nodes and edges. The edges object includes source, target, and type information.
This interface defines the structure for a graph tool object containing metadata and a score. It includes fields for name, category, summary, call summary, and a numeric score.
function::frontend/src/providers/Workspa: This function fetches graph visualization data from the API with a specified depth parameter. It manages loading state and updates the graph data state upon successful retrieval.
This function clears the graph search input and search state variables. It resets the search interface to its initial empty state.
This function triggers a graph reload by sending a POST request to the API. It then calls fetchGraph to refresh the visualization data with the current depth.
This function toggles the inclusion of a specific type in the excluded types set. It adds the type if missing or removes it if already present.
function::frontend/src/components/organi: This component acts as a router that checks the current pathname. It renders the GraphCanvas if the path is "/graph", otherwise it renders the WorkspaceInner component.

This component manages the state for tool selection, interactive mode, and parameter values within the workspace. It renders a list of active groups with their tools, handles tool selection, and displays the selected tool's details and parameters.
This callback function updates the paramValues state with a new key-value pair. It merges the new value into the previous state object.

This component initializes and renders an interactive graph visualization using Cytoscape.js. It handles node styling, layout, click events for selection, and search highlighting based on provided data.
function::frontend/src/lib/stopWords.ts:: This function filters an array of words to remove stop words and short tokens. It keeps only words with a length of at least two characters that are not in the STOP_WORDS set.
function::frontend/src/components/organi: This component renders a UI for exploring a graph with controls for filtering types, depth, and searching. It also displays matched tools based on a task query input.
function::services/neo4j_context.py::get: This function builds a context bundle for a task by querying Neo4j for relevant tools and related files. It uses full-text search to find classes and files, returning their summaries and relevance scores.

This function retrieves the context for a specific file, including its contents, upstream dependencies, and downstream dependents. It performs depth-limited graph traversal to gather relationship summaries and file details.

This function creates a graph-aware context bundle by finding relevant files and traversing their dependencies. It also retrieves relevant tools and provides architecture hints based on the graph structure.
This function returns visualization data containing nodes and edges from the Neo4j graph. It supports filtering by node type and depth, or returns all nodes and edges if no filter is provided.
